Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-21T17:52:44Z","updated_at":"2024-11-21T17:52:44Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

The type hints for `interp` assert that the return dtype is a numpy array, but when the function is called with a scaler it gives a float this raises some mypy errors when using TypeVar.

https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/b85a149fea2c005d395772fcc6078ce370a6bcc4/numpy/lib/_function_base_impl.pyi#L306-L323

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
>>> np.interp(0, [1, 2], [3, 4])
np.float64(3.0)
```


### Error message:

```shell
my_code.py::123 error: Incompatible return value type (got "ndarray[Any, Any]", expected "float")  [return-value]
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

python: 3.12.2
numpy: 2.1.3

### Runtime Environment:

_No response_

---

We've run up against the hardcoded limit of 32 dimensions for `np.array`s. What is the rationale for this limit, and is it possible to increase it? Thanks!
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5744/reactions","total_count":5,"+1":5,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5744/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1614637006","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/5744#issuecomment-1614637006","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5744","id":1614637006,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP85gPWfO","user":{"login":"NoureldinYosri","id":5949112,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU5NDkxMTI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5949112?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri","html_url":"https://github.com/NoureldinYosri","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-06-30T13:14:59Z","updated_at":"2023-06-30T13:14:59Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Are the "old style" iterator planned to be supported or dropped in future versions? If it will be dropped then it doesn't make sense to make it support 64 dimensions.

---

I think a first step would be to provide it through special install as I suggest earlier https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/5744#issuecomment-1613581105 this will allow the people who need this (e.g. quantum computing community) to use and test  the library and see if anything breaks. Eventually the change can be made permanent in numpy v2 as @mattip suggested in https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/5744#issuecomment-1613581105. 

This way we unblock Cirq and similar libraries allowing researchers to simulate quantum systems with more than 32 qubits, while giving time to other libraries to update their code to support the 64 dims if they need to.

What do you think?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1614637006/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

We've run up against the hardcoded limit of 32 dimensions for `np.array`s. What is the rationale for this limit, and is it possible to increase it? Thanks!
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5744/reactions","total_count":5,"+1":5,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5744/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1614831105","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/5744#issuecomment-1614831105","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5744","id":1614831105,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP85gQF4B","user":{"login":"NoureldinYosri","id":5949112,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU5NDkxMTI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5949112?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri","html_url":"https://github.com/NoureldinYosri","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NoureldinYosri/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-06-30T15:37:28Z","updated_at":"2023-06-30T15:37:28Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I built the library with 64 instead of 32 and it works fine
```py3
>> np.ones((1,)*64, dtype=np.complex128)
array([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[1.+0.j]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]])
```

Running the tests, most of them pass. Most of the few that fail seem to be asserting the number of dimensions <= 32 so they should be fine to modify.

<details><summary>Failed Tests</summary>

```
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_conversion_utils.py::TestIntpConverter::test_too_many_dims - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'ValueError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_fancy_indices_special_case - AssertionError: IndexError not raised by __getitem__
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_advanced_indices[1-32-True] - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'IndexError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_advanced_indices[1-32-False] - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'IndexError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_advanced_indices[1-40-True] - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'IndexError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_advanced_indices[1-40-False] - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'IndexError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_advanced_indices[32-32-True] - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'IndexError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_advanced_indices[32-32-False] - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'IndexError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_advanced_indices[32-32-index2] - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'IndexError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_advanced_indices[32-40-True] - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'IndexError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_indexing.py::TestIndexing::test_too_many_advanced_indices[32-40-False] - Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'IndexError'>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_multiarray.py::TestZeroRank::test_invalid_newaxis - AssertionError: IndexError not raised by subscript
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_multiarray.py::TestScalarIndexing::test_invalid_newaxis - AssertionError: IndexError not raised by subscript
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_multiarray.py::TestNewBufferProtocol::test_error_too_many_dims - AssertionError: RuntimeError not raised by array
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_numeric.py::TestBroadcast::test_number_of_arguments - AssertionError: ValueError not raised by broadcast
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_overrides.py::TestGetImplementingArgs::test_too_many_duck_arrays - AssertionError: TypeError not raised
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_overrides.py::TestArrayFunctionImplementation::test_too_many_args - AssertionError: Regex pattern 'maximum number' does not match "no implementation found for 'numpy.core.tests.test_overrides.func' on types that implement __array_function__: ...
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_regression.py::TestRegression::test_array_ndmin_overflow - AssertionError: ValueError not raised by <lambda>
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_regression.py::TestRegression::test_frompyfunc_many_args - AssertionError: ValueError not raised by frompyfunc
FAILED numpy/core/tests/test_umath.py::test_outer_exceeds_maxdims - AssertionError: ValueError not raised
```
</details>

---


I think that having some downstream libraries  support only 32 dimensions not the full 64 dimensions is fine as a starting point. this in addition to replacing `NPY_MAXDIMS` with `NPY_OLD_STYLE_ITER_MAXDIMS` as @seberg suggested should be fine at least to get the ball rolling.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1614831105/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
